title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a wildlife photography walk.
audio_script: Woman: Welcome to our wildlife photography walk. I'm Sophie, and I'll be your guide this morning. The walk is not difficult, but we will stop often to take pictures, so please stay with the group. We will leave from the visitor centre at [14]quarter past nine[/14]. If anyone still needs to use the toilets, please do that now. Our first stop will be near the old stone bridge, where birds often feed in the shallow water. The bird we most hope to see today is the [15]kingfisher[/15]. It is small and very fast, so you need to be quiet. After that, we will follow the path through the wood. The ground may be muddy, so strong [16]boots[/16] are better than ordinary shoes. We will have a short break beside the [17]lake[/17], where you can eat your snack. Please take all rubbish home with you. In the second part of the walk, I will show you how to photograph insects on flowers. A useful thing to bring is a small [18]mirror[/18], because it can help move light onto dark places. At the end, anyone who wants more advice can come to a free class next [19]Friday[/19] at the centre. Please remember that we are walking through a protected area, so do not pick flowers or leave the path without asking me first. You do not need an expensive camera for today. A simple camera or phone is fine, as the aim is to practise looking carefully. Please check the notice board before you leave, because small changes to rooms or times may be written there. If you are not sure about anything, ask a member of staff.

items.text_before: Useful thing to bring: a small

questions.explanation: The speaker says they will leave at quarter past nine.
The speaker says they hope to see the kingfisher.
The speaker says strong boots are better than ordinary shoes.
The speaker says they will have a short break beside the lake.

questions.prompt: Useful thing to bring: a small
questions.explanation: The speaker says a small mirror is useful for moving light.
The speaker says the free class is next Friday.
